description:
## The change

One line in `src/types.rs`:

```rust
pub fn is_equiv(&self, other: &Descr) -> bool {
    self == other || (self.is_subtype(other) && other.is_subtype(self))
}
```

Structural equality (`self == other`, via the derived `PartialEq`)
is a *sufficient* condition for semantic equivalence: two `Descr`
values with identical fields denote the same set of values. So
short-circuiting to `true` on structural match is sound by
construction — we only ever return `true` earlier than before,
never wrongly.

## Why this is the right shape

The fast-path goes inside the kernel, not at the callsites. Every
existing caller (`type_fn`'s var-level fixpoint at `ir_typer.rs:1220`,
env propagation at `:1255`, block-level join at `:1359`,
`compute_effective_returns` at `:2548`, the four other scattered
sites) benefits automatically. No `is_same_value()` helper, no
parallel cheap-check at each callsite, no leak of "this is an
optimization" into surrounding code.

## Why this is huge

Measured on `ast_eval`: per-`type_fn` cost is ~388 µs vs ~65 µs on
`fib_tailrec`. The 6× difference is `is_equiv` doing semantic
subtyping (DNF intersection-with-negation, coinductive emptiness
walk) on tuple-shaped Descrs that are *bit-identical* across iters
in the common case — the inner fixpoint is converging, which means
"unchanged" is the common answer.

## Acceptance

- One-line change in `Descr::is_equiv`.
- All existing typer tests pass unchanged.
- `ast_eval` `type_module` single-call time drops from ~172 ms to
  the ballpark of 25 ms (measure before/after with a release build).

## Out of scope

- Canonicalizing `Descr` operations to make structural equality
  match semantic equality more often. Fast-path misses still fall
  through to the slow path — correctness is unaffected.
- Fast-paths inside `is_subtype` / `is_empty` (separate question).

## Independent of

TYP.2 and TYP.3. Lands first because it's the smallest verifiable
win and unlocks better measurements for the other two.
title: fz-TYP.1: Descr::is_equiv structural fast-path
